data.title: Intelligent Wave's Nine-Month Profit Flat, Anticipates 12% Profit Growth for Full Year
data.description:
Intelligent Wave reported profit of 1.02 billion yen for the nine months ended March, down 0.3% from a year earlier, according to a Wednesday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

Earnings per share held steady at 39.13 yen, compared with 39.16 yen in the prior-year period. Net sales rose 7.2% to 11.53 billion yen.

The software company expects profit for the full fiscal year ending June to grow 11.9% to 1.59 billion yen, with earnings per share projected at 60.50 yen. Net sales are forecast to rise 10.2% to 16 billion yen.

Intelligent Wave expects a year-end payout of 20 yen, down from 25 yen a year ago.

data.title: Infratil Unlikely to Be Long-Term Shareholder of Contact EnergyMarket Talk
data.description: What Infratil decides to do with a 10% stake in Contact Energy will be interesting, says Forsyth Barr. Infratil will have the equity interest, currently worth around NZ$900 million, once Contact's takeover of Manawa Energy completes. Analyst Aaron Ibbotson doesn't see it as a long-term holding for Infratil. "Infratil has two more mature assets in the NZ$1 billion size bracket--Wellington International Airport and Diagnostic Imaging," Forsyth Barr. "Both assets are too small to matter to Infratil now but too big to ignore." Forsyth Barr expects Infratil to consider strategic options for both. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Global Energy Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2048 ET - What Infratil decides to do with a 10% stake in Contact Energy will be interesting, says Forsyth Barr. Infratil will have the equity interest, currently worth around NZ$900 million, once Contact's takeover of Manawa Energy completes. Analyst Aaron Ibbotson doesn't see it as a long-term holding for Infratil. "Infratil has two more mature assets in the NZ$1 billion size bracket--Wellington International Airport and Diagnostic Imaging," Forsyth Barr. "Both assets are too small to matter to Infratil now but too big to ignore." Forsyth Barr expects Infratil to consider strategic options for both. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2007 ET - Oil consolidates in early Asian trade. However, data released overnight by the EIA showed commercial crude oil stocks excluding Strategic Petroleum Reserve dropped by 2 million barrels in week ended May 2. An analyst survey by The Wall Street Journal had predicted a 1.3 million barrel decline in crude stockpiles. Crude oil prices have been volatile as traders are still worried over OPEC+'s production policy, Pepperstone's Quasar Elizundia says in an email. OPEC+ is set to quicken production increases, heightening oversupply risks, the research strategist adds. Front-month WTI crude oil futures are 0.1% higher at $58.13/bbl; front-month Brent crude oil futures are steady at $61.13/bbl. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1809 ET - Uncertainties about the Trump administration's clean-energy policies are not slowing down the industry's expansion outside the U.S., Jim Coulter, executive chairman of TPG, says during an earnings call. "They seem unbothered by the discussion that is going on about [the] U. S. policy," Coulter says of overseas markets. He points to the more than $2 trillion the world spent in clean-energy development last year, double the spending across fossil-fuel sectors. He also mentions the recently agreed acquisition by a TPG-led group of investors of a 90% stake in Siemens Gamesa's offshore wind business in India. "Around the world, north of 90% of the energy addition was happening through clean energy," he says. "[Those] markets are continuing apace and if anything, accelerating." (luis.garcia@wsj.com; @lhvgarcia)

1622 ET - The takeaway from Jerome Powell's May press conference: Don't hold your breath for the Fed to adjust interest rates, writes economist Stephen Stanley of Santander. Markets, eager to quickly incorporate revised expectations for how the Fed will set rates going forward, need to take a deep breath, he says. "Financial market participants want this to be a comic book, where we get to the end of the story in 10 minutes, but Powell is suggesting that it is an 800-page novel, and we are currently reading Chapter 2," Stanley writes in a note to clients. "So, buckle in and keep reading." (matt.grossman@wsj.com, @mattgrossman)

1451 ET - Crude futures retreat after yesterday's technical rebound as concerns about rising OPEC+ production outweigh positive signs of U.S.-China trade talks and a second weekly draw in U.S. crude stocks. Most of the moving parts in the EIA inventory data were on the bearish side, except for the 98,000 barrels-a-day drop in U.S. production, Mizuho's Robert Yawger says ina note, pointing to higher imports, lower exports, and virtually no change in inputs to refineries. Oil prices slipped a bit further after the Fed left interest rates unchanged and said uncertainty about the economic outlook has increased. WTI settles down 1.7% at $58.07 a barrel, and Brent falls 1.7% to $61.12. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1437 ET - Natural gas futures rise as LNG feedgas flows pick up after an outage at Freeport LNG and the market looks toward warming weather to lift demand in weeks ahead. On Thursday the EIA is expected to report a 101 Bcfinjection into storage for last week, a second straight triple-digit build, according to a Wall Street Journal survey of analysts. "Prices appear range-bound in the near future discounting a surprise in storage," Gelber & Associates says in a note. "High injections and mild weather skew bearish, though the spread is tightening as we head into summer where hot weather and bullish storage surprises could have outsized price impacts." Nymex natural gas settles up 4.6% at $3.621/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1227 ET - Crude futures turn lower after a steady start, returning some of the previous day's gains as OPEC+ production plans keep buyers cautious. A tentative start to U.S.-China talks this week is seen supportive of oil, as are comments from U.S. producers about curtailing drilling because of low prices. The EIA reported a 2 million barrel decline in crude oil stockpiles for last week, while gasoline stocks rose for the first time in more than two months. WTI and Brent are down 1.3% at $58.31 and $61.34 a barrel, respectively. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1112 ET - Corporate bonds within the U.S. energy sector look vulnerable if oil prices fall further, UBS credit strategists say in a note. WTI currently trades at $58.76 a barrel, well below levels above $74 at the start of April due to President Trump's tariff announcements and OPEC+'s recent decision to increase output. U.S. energy bonds within both investment-grade and high yield "are underperforming and we remain cautious," the strategists say, adding that the combination of OPEC's production hikes and tariffs could lower WTI prices to near $50. Spreads on these bonds "look underpriced" for the risk of further oil price declines, they say. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0946 ET - Jefferies economist Thomas Simons extends his sympathies to hard-working members of the Federal Reserve press corps: "It is difficult to imagine how they can come up with questions that Powell can and will provide a straight answer to regarding the outlook for policy or the economy," Simons writes. Simons expects Fed Chair Jerome Powell to play it close to the vest at this afternoon's press conference, maintaining a similar tone from his last presser in March. Tariff-induced inflation remains a concern, but beyond that, "we see a balanced view on the dual mandate that does not express limitation in the Fed's capacity to provide accommodation if the growth and labor market outlook were to weaken materially," Simons writes. (matt.grossman@wsj.com, @mattgrossman)

0931 ET - Oil futures are holding their ground after yesterday's rebound, supported by plans for trade talks this week between U.S. and Chinese officials and an industry report of a large U.S. crude stock draw. "The energy complex appears to be pricing in less tariff-related demand deterioration than previously expected," Ritterbusch says in a note. The API's report of a 4.5 million barrel crude inventory draw for last week, "especially given the 0.9 million barrel reduction in Cushing supply," is supportive of crude, the firm adds. Closely watched EIA inventory data are due at 10:30 a.m. ET. WTI is trading flat at $59.09 a barrel, and Brent is off 0.1% at $62.11 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0855 ET - Natural gas futures pick up from the previous day's losses as LNG feedgas flows recover and the approach of summer heat is seen likely to lift demand in weeks ahead. "Essentially, weather patterns aren't far from being bullish as early season heat teases this summer will in fact be hotter than normal," NatGasWeather.com says in a note. On the downside, the market faces a series of large inventory builds that are expected to increase the surplus over the five-year average, while leaving storage well below year-earlier levels. Nymex natural gas is up 4.2% at $3.610/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0659 ET - German manufacturing orders easily beat consensus in March, though trade uncertainty will weigh on activity ahead, Pantheon Macroeconomics' Claus Vistesen says in an note. Frontloading by U.S. importers ahead of tariffs likely contributed, though the scale of the impact is difficult to quantify, he says. Pharmaceutical orders jumped 17%, partly reflecting efforts by U.S. imports to get ahead of likely tariffs in the sector. But trade concerns will continue to weigh on investment, even as exports perform strongly and consumption growth slows only marginally. There is an upside risk, however, should firms look through trade concerns on greater domestic political clarity under the new government led by Chancellor Merz, he says. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

data.title: First Majestic Silver Corp reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• First Majestic Silver Corp reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 5 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -6 cents. The mean expectation of five analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 6 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 4 cents to 8 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 130.1% to $243.94 million from a year ago; analysts expected $241.00 million.

  • First Majestic Silver Corp shares had fallen by 4.5% this quarter and gained 16.4% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 29.1% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, two analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the non-gold precious metals & minerals peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for First Majestic Silver Corp is C$13.62

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data May 8 at 12:47 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. (For questions concerning the data in this report, contact Estimates.Support@lseg.com. For any other questions or feedback, contact RefinitivNewsSupport@thomsonreuters.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2049 ET - Briscoe's 1Q trading update was a mixed bag for Forsyth Barr. Sales were slightly softer than analyst Paul Laxton Koraua had expected, but half-year net profit guidance of NZ$30 million implies a better first-half gross margin outlook than thought. Forsyth Barr notes that Briscoe is committed to maintaining the same level of profitability as last year, which signals a seasonal recovery through 2H. "The next key news flow will be Briscoe's annual shareholder meeting on May 15, where the company typically provides a more fulsome update," Forsyth Barr says. "On our revised earnings, Briscoe is trading on circa 14x 12-month forward PE--slightly above historic levels." (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2048 ET - What Infratil decides to do with a 10% stake in Contact Energy will be interesting, says Forsyth Barr. Infratil will have the equity interest, currently worth around NZ$900 million, once Contact's takeover of Manawa Energy completes. Analyst Aaron Ibbotson doesn't see it as a long-term holding for Infratil. "Infratil has two more mature assets in the NZ$1 billion size bracket--Wellington International Airport and Diagnostic Imaging," Forsyth Barr. "Both assets are too small to matter to Infratil now but too big to ignore." Forsyth Barr expects Infratil to consider strategic options for both. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2044 ET - Contact Energy will have plenty of options once its takeover of Manawa Energy completes, but its balance sheet may need some help, suggests Forsyth Barr. Contact's combination with Manawa creates New Zealand's second-largest power generator, and analyst Andrew Harvey-Green says the size of its development pipeline also ranks second in the sector. "However, with Contact taking on additional debt, its net debt/Ebitdaf will be well above 3x in FY 2026 and FY 2027, meaning funding more developments on balance sheet will be difficult without an equity raise," Forsyth Barr says.(david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2036 ET - Bega Cheese is advancing toward a target of A$250 million of Ebitda by FY 2028, and Bell Potter sees the potential for a stepchange in its share price as these efforts take hold. Bega Cheese's latest move involves consolidating its Strathmerton cheese processing and packaging operations into its existing site in Bega. Bell Potter has a buy call on Bega Cheese and a A$7.00/share price target, but thinks momentum could propel it higher. "If successful in delivering on these initiatives and sustaining its historical 10-year average trading multiple then the upside remains compelling, with a likely target of A$8.00-A$8.50/share," analyst Jonathan Snape says. Bega Cheese is down 0.2% at A$5.92. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2028 ET - While sales trends slowed in every division of Super Retail since its February update, Jefferies takes heart from signs that the auto market is becoming more rational after a period of intense competition. Supercheap Auto's like-for-like sales are down 0.1% in fiscal 2H so far, but management said there was some evidence of stability in April. Analyst Michael Simotas points out that April is always promotional in auto, but activity became more rational. Overall, Jefferies said the key disappointment in Super Retail's update was material unallocated costs in FY 2026. "While they should be one-off, FY 2026 net profit is likely to decline for the third year, which combined with Sports Direct overhang likely constrains share performance," the bank says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2027 ET - Japanese stocks are lower as uncertainty over U.S. tariffs and their economic implications continues. Shippers and trading houses are leading the declines. Mitsui O.S.K. Lines is down 2.8% and Mitsui & Co. is 1.9% lower. USD/JPY is at 143.71, compared with 142.95 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Earnings are in focus, with Toyota Motor scheduled to announce its results later in the day. The Nikkei Stock Average is down 0.1% at 36736.55. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

1951 ET - Japanese stocks may trade rangebound as uncertainty over U.S. tariffs and their economic implications continues. Nikkei futures are up 0.1% at 36860 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 143.77, compared with 142.95 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Earnings are in focus, with Toyota Motor set to announce its results later in the day. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 0.1% to 36779.66 on Wednesday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1855 ET - National Australia Bank has a stronger earnings outlook than Westpac and enjoys far more valuation support than Commonwealth Bank, Morgan Stanley analysts say. Its relative attractiveness compared with its peers helps keep the analysts equal-weight on the stock following what they tell clients in a note was a first-half result with no surprises. Key operating trends were in line with their expectations, which the MS team says helps alleviate concerns about underlying margins, credit quality and capital that had emerged with NAB's 1Q update. They lift their target price 2.9%, to A$35.00. Shares are at A$35.87 ahead of the open. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

1841 ET [Dow Jones]--Australia's S&P/ASX 200 looks set to edge higher at the open after the Federal Reserve held interest rates and signaled it would wait before deciding on future movements. ASX futures are up by 0.1% ahead of Thursday's session, suggesting a muted start to the day. The benchmark index edged 0.3% higher Wednesday, paring its losses so far this week to 0.7%. Ahead of the open, ANZ bank reported flat first-half cash earnings and a steady interim dividend. Toll-road operator Transurban said it would lay off 300 staff in a restructuring that will require a provision and expense in its end-of-year result. In the U.S., the DJIA rose 0.7%, the S&P 500 added 0.4% and the Nasdaq Composite lifted 0.3%. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

1809 ET - Uncertainties about the Trump administration's clean-energy policies are not slowing down the industry's expansion outside the U.S., Jim Coulter, executive chairman of TPG, says during an earnings call. "They seem unbothered by the discussion that is going on about [the] U. S. policy," Coulter says of overseas markets. He points to the more than $2 trillion the world spent in clean-energy development last year, double the spending across fossil-fuel sectors. He also mentions the recently agreed acquisition by a TPG-led group of investors of a 90% stake in Siemens Gamesa's offshore wind business in India. "Around the world, north of 90% of the energy addition was happening through clean energy," he says. "[Those] markets are continuing apace and if anything, accelerating." (luis.garcia@wsj.com; @lhvgarcia)

1738 ET - Applovin's 2Q outlook calls for advertising revenue to grow 3.4% to 5.2% sequentially, marking a slowdown from the 16% sequential growth the unit logged in the recent quarter. CEO Adam Foroughi attributes this slowdown largely to seasonality. "Our seasons are tied to time spent on mobile devices," he says on a call with analysts, noting that 1Q benefits from the winter holidays, Ramadan and Spring Break. "In 2Q, you actually don't have almost anything, as only the tail end gets in the summer," Foroughi says, adding that he expects an uptick in sequential growth in both 3Q and 4Q. Shares rise 14% after hours. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1605 ET - U.S. stocks rise after a choppy session in which the Fed held rates steady, while warning that the economy faced growing risks of higher unemployment and higher inflation due to tariff increases. Stock gains are supported by reports that China and the U.S. are finally ready to talk trade, which investors hope will end in the rollback of punitive tit-for-tat tariffs. Disney, meanwhile, gains 11% after posting strong quarterly results and saying it was planning a new theme park in Abu Dhabi. DJIA gains 284 points, or 0.7% to 41113, the S&P 500 rises 0.4% to 5631 and the Nasdaq adds 0.3% to 17738. (patrick.sullivan@wsj.com)

data.title: Australia's Orica jumps as traders cheer earnings outlook, higher dividend
data.description:
** Shares of Orica Ltd rise 7.4% to A$18.01, their highest point since December 13

** Stock is top gainer in the benchmark index

** The infrastructure solutions firm says it expects FY205 EBIT to increase y/y despite posting a HY statutory net loss after tax of A$89 million ($57.24 million)

** Declares interim dividend of 0.25 Australian cents per share vs 0.19 Australian cents per share last year

** Stock posts biggest intraday percentage gain since November 9, 2022

** Stock up 8.2% YTD, including moves in current session

($1 = 1.5550 Australian dollars)

data.description: A Polymerupdate source in East Africa reported that a producer from Saudi Arabia is heard to have announced its offer for LLDPE Film (318BJ) grade at the USD 1200/mt levels.This offer is for May 2025 shipment on CFR Beira port basis.

data.title: Recent Low density polyethylene (LDPE Film) offers in Mozambique
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in East Africa stated that a producer from Saudi Arabia is heard to have offered its LDPE Film (HP2022JN 149/HP2023JN 149) grades at the USD 1320/mt levels.This offer is for May 2025 shipment on CFR Beira port basis.

data.title: Soybeans Hits 4-week Low
data.description:
Soybeans decreased to a 4-week low of 1028.00 USd/Bu.

Over the past 4 weeks, Soybeans gained 1.61%, and in the last 12 months, it decreased 13.78%.

data.title: R C M Technologies Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• R C M Technologies Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 54 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 48 cents. The lone analyst forecast for the quarter was for earnings of 55 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 17.4% to $84.47 million from a year ago; analysts expected $77.80 million.

  • R C M Technologies Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 54 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $4.19 million.

  • R C M Technologies Inc shares had risen by 10.4% this quarter and lost 22.3% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 1.8% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the employment services peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for R C M Technologies Inc is $30.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data May 8 at 12:50 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Telix Says Publication of Thyroid Cancer Therapy Candidate in American Thyroid Association Journal Validates Safety Profile
data.description:
Telix Pharmaceuticals said Thursday that its fibroblast-activation-protein-targeting therapy candidate, TLX400, was published in Thyroid, the official journal of the American Thyroid Association, providing further validation of the safety profile and potential efficacy of TLX400.

TLX400 showed an "encouraging" safety profile and efficacy in aggressive, radioiodine-resistant thyroid cancer, according to a statement. It has been in-licensed by Telix as part of a portfolio of next-generation therapeutic and diagnostic radiopharmaceutical candidates.

"In the cohort of patients with reported efficacy, the treatment demonstrated median overall survival and progression-free survival durations of 32 months and 29 months, with a promising 50% partial response rate," Sanjana Ballal, investigator and lead author noted.

However, potential risks, including significant side effects, require careful management, particularly in patients with comorbidities such as cardiac issues, hypertension, stroke, and kidney dysfunction, who may not be optimal candidates for tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy, Telix said.

The company's shares slipped almost 1% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Apple asks US appeals court to pause ruling in Epic Games case
data.description:
Apple has asked a federal appeals court to temporarily pause key provisions in a U.S. judge’s ruling that ordered the tech company to immediately open its lucrative App Store to more competition.

Apple told the San Francisco-based 9th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals in a filing on Wednesday that it will be irreparably harmed if the April 30 order is not put on hold while the iPhone maker’s legal challenge is pending.

Apple is fighting a ruling that found the company in contempt of an earlier order in a 2020 antitrust lawsuit brought by Epic Games, maker of the online video game Fortnite.

In its filing, Apple said the new ruling blocks the company from "exercising control over core aspects of its business operations."

U.S. District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers ordered Apple to end several practices that she said were designed to circumvent the injunction. Apple's filing focused on two of them, including the court's ban on a new 27% fee Apple imposed on app developers when its customers complete an app purchase outside the App Store.

Apple in its filing said a federal court can't "force Apple to permanently give away free access to its products and services."

Apple is also challenging part of the judge's order that bars the company from restricting where developers place links to make purchases outside of an app.

Epic Games did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

In the underlying lawsuit, Epic Games sued Apple to loosen its control over transactions in applications that use its iOS operating system and how apps are distributed to consumers.

The Cupertino, California-based company willfully failed to comply with a 2021 injunction in the case designed to allow developers to more easily steer consumers to potentially cheaper non-Apple payment options, Gonzalez Rogers said in her decision.

"Apple sought to maintain a revenue stream worth billions in direct defiance of this court’s injunction,” Gonzalez Rogers wrote.

Gonzalez Rogers said Apple had misled the court about its efforts to comply with her injunction and referred the company and one of its executives to federal prosecutors for a possible criminal contempt investigation.

data.title: U.S. to Overhaul Curbs on AI Chip Exports After Industry Backlash
data.description:
By Liza Lin and Amrith Ramkumar

The Trump administration plans to overhaul controversial regulations that would limit how many artificial-intelligence chips individual countries can buy, giving companies such as Nvidia a potential reprieve from tight export controls.

The Commerce Department plans to replace the rule, which imposed caps on how many chips could go to countries such as India, Switzerland, Mexico and Israel, a spokeswoman said. "The Biden AI rule is overly complex, overly bureaucratic and would stymie American innovation," she said.

The decision is at least a temporary boon for tech titans that have fought the rules and been buffeted by a wave of export restrictions in recent years. Seamless chip sales to countries friendly with the U.S. are critical to their businesses but would be jeopardized by the new regulations, they have said.

Nvidia shares rose by more than 3% as news surfaced of the administration's plans.

Many industry executives are still bracing for tight export controls that take a different form from the Biden team's proposal. The Trump administration recently cracked down on Nvidia selling a specialized AI chip to China, prompting the company to record a $5.5 billion charge related to the change.

The rule, commonly known as AI diffusion, had been set to go into effect May 15. Focus will now turn to the Trump team's plan to remake it, which could take up to a couple of months, people familiar with the matter said. Policymakers are weighing how to block adversaries such as China from accessing advanced chips without hurting American technology companies.

Thursday's announcement came ahead of Trump's planned visit to the Middle East. Countries such as Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates also would have faced limits on chip purchases under the Biden-era rule, fueling speculation that the administration will discuss access to advanced chips on the trip.

"We might be doing that, yeah," Trump said Wednesday when asked by reporters about easing chip restrictions. "And it will be announced soon." The U.A.E. announced a $1.4 trillion commitment to invest in the U.S. in late March.

The delays in finalizing restrictions have been destabilizing across the industry, experts say. Some nations were likely pulling forward chip purchases ahead of the rule's implementation, said Lennart Heim, a researcher at RAND. Heim analyzed trade data from Taiwan and said it showed a large increase in shipments of data processing equipment that likely included A.I. chips from Taiwan to Malaysia in March.

The total value of such exports hit $1.87 billion in March, up several-fold from a year ago, Heim said.

Fears that other countries would route U.S. chips to adversaries such as China led to the diffusion rule, which was announced in the final days of the Biden administration. It built on previous restrictions on the sale of advanced American chips to China that began in 2022. Lawmakers and national-security officials have said export controls are key to maintaining America's advantage over rival nations in AI.

Tech companies such as Microsoft and Oracle quickly protested the diffusion rule, arguing it would deprive American companies of business opportunities abroad without doing much to hamper China, the main target.

The system would have split countries into three different buckets. One relatively small group of U.S. friends and allies could freely purchase advanced AI chips from the United States. Adversaries, including China and Iran, would be barred from buying the chips. The third category included all other countries, an expansive group that includes many of the friendly nations. They would be limited in how many chips they could buy.

In addition, sharing data that underpins the most sophisticated AI systems would have required approval from the Commerce Department.

In recent weeks, the Trump administration has considered scrapping the tiered system in favor of bilateral agreements with individual countries, people familiar with the matter said.

The Trump administration has also put chip-export issues on the table in trade talks with some countries, pressuring them to commit to helping the U.S. keep chips out of the hands of adversaries in exchange for more favorable tariffs, the people said.

Write to Liza Lin at liza.lin@wsj.com and Amrith Ramkumar at amrith.ramkumar@wsj.com

data.title: Contact Energy May Need to Raise Equity to Fund DevelopmentsMarket Talk
data.description: Contact Energy will have plenty of options once its takeover of Manawa Energy completes, but its balance sheet may need some help, suggests Forsyth Barr. Contact's combination with Manawa creates New Zealand's second-largest power generator, and analyst Andrew Harvey-Green says the size of its development pipeline also ranks second in the sector. "However, with Contact taking on additional debt, its net debt/Ebitdaf will be well above 3x in FY 2026 and FY 2027, meaning funding more developments on balance sheet will be difficult without an equity raise," Forsyth Barr says.(david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Australia's Pro Medicus hits near 8-week high on securing $12.9 million contract
data.description:
** Shares of Australia's Pro Medicus rise as much as 4% to A$246.760, their highest levels since March 11

** Stock among top gainer in the benchmark index

** PME posts its biggest intraday pct gain since April 28

** The medical imaging platforms maker signs a A$20 million ($12.86 million), five-year contract with the University of Iowa Health Care

** Co to provide cloud-based Visage 7 Enterprise Imaging Platform

** Stock has fallen 5.2% so far this year

($1 = 1.5550 Australian dollars)

data.title: Transurban Group Cuts 300 Jobs
data.description:
Transurban Group will be cutting 300 jobs to save over AU$50 million in annualized savings, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing., according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

A part of these savings will be re-allocated into areas of customer-facing and operational technologies, the filing said.

The expenses related to the job cuts will be accounted for in its full fiscal year results and will be excluded from proportional operating earnings before interest, tax, depreciation, and amortization.

The company has reiterated its fiscal year cost and distribution guidance.

data.title: Asian Currencies Consolidate; May Be Weighed by Fed OutlookMarket Talk
data.description: Asian currencies mostly consolidate in early trade, but may be weighed by prospects that the Fed could wait longer to cut rates. FOMC's statement noted that 'risks of higher unemployment and higher inflation have risen,' CBA's Carol Kong says in a research report. Also, Fed Chair Powell said the central bank is 'in the right place to wait and see' where the economy is headed to determine the right monetary policy response, the economist and currency strategist says. "Powell's comments suggest the risk is the FOMC will resume its rate cutting cycle later than our forecast of July," Kong adds. USD/JPY edges 0.1% lower to 143.72. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: TruBridge Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• TruBridge Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 36 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 19 cents. The mean expectation of four analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 29 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 9 cents to 40 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 4.8% to $87.21 million from a year ago; analysts expected $86.21 million.

  • TruBridge Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 3 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $459 thousand.

  • TruBridge Inc shares had fallen by 7.5% this quarter and gained 29.1% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 1.7% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the advanced medical equipment & technology peer group is "hold."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for TruBridge Inc is $28.50

data.title: Bega Cheese's Earnings Drive Excites BullMarket Talk
data.description: Bega Cheese is advancing toward a target of A$250 million of Ebitda by FY 2028, and Bell Potter sees the potential for a stepchange in its share price as these efforts take hold. Bega Cheese's latest move involves consolidating its Strathmerton cheese processing and packaging operations into its existing site in Bega. Bell Potter has a buy call on Bega Cheese and a A$7.00/share price target, but thinks momentum could propel it higher. "If successful in delivering on these initiatives and sustaining its historical 10-year average trading multiple then the upside remains compelling, with a likely target of A$8.00-A$8.50/share," analyst Jonathan Snape says. Bega Cheese is down 0.2% at A$5.92. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Aspen Insurance Prices Upsized Initial Public Offering of 13.25M Class A Shares at $30.00 Each >AHL
Japanese Equities Climb After US Fed Holds Rates, Powell Signals Caution on Cuts
data.description:
Japanese equities opened higher on Thursday, tracking gains on Wall Street, after the Federal Reserve maintained interest rates and signalled confidence in the US economy while downplaying imminent rate cuts.

The Nikkei 225 rose 83.7 points to 36,863.38 in early trading.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell reassured investors following the Federal Open Market Committee's decision to hold rates steady, though he noted increasing risks of higher unemployment and inflation.

The Fed emphasized a cautious approach, preferring to keep rates unchanged until there is more clarity on the economic outlook, particularly after President Donald Trump's recent tariff announcements.

ANZ falls as Australian lender's gross impaired assets jump 48%

** YTD, ANZ Group shares up 4%

data.title: Barton Gold launches environmental studies at Tunkillia as transition to producer accelerates
data.description: Barton Gold’s (OTCQB: BGDFF) major 2025 commercialisation focus in accelerating its large-scale Tunkillia project in South Australia is underway with the commencement of environmental studies. The Tunkillia project forms part of Barton’s two-pronged development focus in 2025 as it leads the re-emergence of the SA gold sector. The critical Tunkillia environmental scoping work has kicked off the project’s pre-feasibility study and mining lease (ML) programs. Optimised scoping study Barton’s acceleration of long-lead feasibility work programs for Tunkillia follows the recent publication of the project’s optimised scoping study (OSS). The OSS estimated Tunkillia can provide operating free cash flow of $2.7 billion, net present value of $1.4b and an internal rate of return of 73%. The study also found that material efficiencies had extended the project’s total life (including construction) to approximately 10 years and accelerated higher-value mill feed over an eight-year life-of-mine. Environmental analysis Barton has brought in the ERIAS Group to prepare an environmental ‘scoping report’, the first step in the ML approval process. The report will set out the scope of works for Tunkillia environmental assessments as agreed upon by the SA Department for Energy and Mining. “Tunkillia’s new OSS has confirmed a large-scale, competitive gold and silver operation with significant economies of scale offering strong financial and capital leverage to a rapidly evolving gold market,” managing director Alexander Scanlon said. “We are accelerating Tunkillia in parallel with studies for Stage 1 operations leveraging our Central Gawler Mill,” Mr Scanlon said. Operating free cash Barton has forecast the ‘starter pit’ at Tunkillia will produce $825 million of operating free cash at a margin of more than $4,000 per ounce during the first 13 months of operations. Mr Scanlon said this puts Barton in a very strong position to advance project studies, approvals, financing and development. “Our goal is to transition to producer from 2026, generate cash flows and then use these to develop Tunkillia as our Stage 2 expansion project for a lower-cost, lower-risk and lower-dilution pathway to 150,000 ounces per annum gold.” “We are wasting no time in doing so and are well positioned with a $12m track record of continuing asset monetisation, a low net cash burn rate and $7m cash on hand.”

data.title: Apollo's Aspen Insurance raises $397.5 million in US IPO
data.description: Aspen Insurance priced its New York initial public offering within its marketed range on Wednesday, raising $397.5 million for the Bermuda-based specialty insurer.

data.title: Shareholders of Perpetua Resources Corp. Should Contact Levi & Korsinsky Before May 20, 2025 to Discuss Your Rights - PPTA
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 7, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Perpetua Resources Corp. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/perpetua-resources-corp-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=147453&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

<img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/api/iframe.ashx?releaseid=1025280&url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fembed%2fv1NaUHVSJbE" width="0"/></div></div></div><p><strong>THE LAWSUIT:</strong> A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Perpetua Resources Corp. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between April 17, 2024 and February 13, 2025.</p><p><strong>CASE DETAILS:</strong> According to the complaint, defendants provided investors with material information concerning Perpetua's expected initial capital expenditure for the Stibnite Gold Project. Defendants' statements included, among other things, minimization of the impact of inflation and other potential sources for increased capital expenditure costs for the project.</p><p>On February 13, 2025, Perpetua published an updated cash flow model for the Stibnite Gold Project, unveiling additional capital expenses of $952 million, a more than 75% increase from the original figures presented to investors and well beyond the suggested 10-20% increase contemplated by defendants. The Company attributed these increased costs on inflation, indirect costs, higher mining costs, and direct decisions defendants made with respect to the project, including the choice to change the design of the electrical poles from timber to steel and the decision to "buy-and-build instead of lease the oxygen plant."</p><p>Following this news, the price of Perpetua's common stock declined dramatically. From a closing market price of $11.97 per share on February 13, 2025, Perpetua's stock price fell to $9.29 per share on February 14, 2025, a decline of about 22.39% in the span of just a single day.</p><p><strong>WHAT'S NEXT?</strong> If you suffered a loss in Perpetua Resources Corp. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to <a href="https://pr.report/a825" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/pslra-1/perpetua-resources-corp-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=147453&wire=1</a> to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. <strong>There is no cost or obligation to participate.</strong></p><p><strong>WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY:</strong> Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.</p><p><strong>CONTACT:</strong><br/>Levi & Korsinsky, LLP<br/>Joseph E. Levi, Esq.<br/>Ed Korsinsky, Esq.<br/>33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor<br/>New York, NY 10004<br/><a href="mailto:jlevi@levikorsinsky.com" rel="nofollow">jlevi@levikorsinsky.com</a><br/>Tel: (212) 363-7500<br/>Fax: (212) 363-7171<br/><a href="https://pr.report/a826" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/</a></p><p><strong>SOURCE:</strong> Levi & Korsinsky, LLP</p><br/><br/>View the original <a href="https://www.accessnewswire.com/newsroom/en/business-and-professional-services/shareholders-of-perpetua-resources-corp.-should-contact-levi-and-1025280">press release</a> on ACCESS Newswire<br/><br/><img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/img.ashx?id=1025280" width="0"/></body></html>
